title: paspalum michauxianum
intro: Paspalum michauxianum, also known as Michaux's paspalum, is a type of perennial grass native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to soothe and calm irritated skin. The health benefits of P. michauxianum include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing relief from skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. P. michauxianum can be prepared as a herbal tea, decoction, or tincture, and is often used topically to apply directly to affected areas of the skin.
